[the core task is to find the maximum value of the function u = log_{1/2}(8xy + 4y² + 1) under the constraints x ≥ 0, y ≥ 0, and x + 2y = 1/2]  
[the constraints x ≥ 0, y ≥ 0, and x + 2y = 1/2 define a region in the xy-plane where x and y are non-negative and their sum is fixed]  
[the function u involves a logarithm with base 1/2, which is a decreasing function, and the argument of the logarithm is 8xy + 4y² + 1, which is a quadratic expression in x and y]  
[the expression 8xy + 4y² + 1 can be simplified by substituting x = 1/2 - 2y into the equation to express it entirely in terms of y]  
[after substitution, the expression becomes 8(1/2 - 2y)y + 4y² + 1, which simplifies to 4y - 16y² + 4y² + 1 = 4y - 12y² + 1]  
[the maximum value of u is achieved when the argument of the logarithm is minimized, since the base of the logarithm is less than 1, making the function u decreasing]  
[the expression 4y - 12y² + 1 is a quadratic function in y, and its maximum value occurs at the vertex of the parabola]  
[the vertex of the quadratic expression 4y - 12y² + 1 is at y = -b/(2a) = -4/(2*(-12)) = 1/6]  
[substituting y = 1/6 into the expression gives 4*(1/6) - 12*(1/6)² + 1 = 2/3 - 12*(1/36) + 1 = 2/3 - 1/3 + 1 = 1]  
[the maximum value of the argument of the logarithm is 1, so u = log_{1/2}(1) = 0]  
[the maximum value of u is 0, which corresponds to option B]
